channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Vicente Gonzalez and Mayra Flores clash in fiery South Texas congressional debate
item.description: In their only debate before Election Day, Gonzalez and Flores traded jabs over the economy, immigration and abortion, and regularly spoke over each other.
item.dc:creator: Texas Tribune, By Berenice Garcia, KPRC 2

item.title: Republicans appeal a Georgia judge’s ruling that invalidates seven election rules
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — National and state Republicans on Thursday appealed a judge's ruling that said seven election rules recently passed by Georgia's State Election Board are “illegal, unconstitutional and void.
item.dc:creator: KATE BRUMBACK and JEFF AMY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Lydia Dominguez talks leadership, safety, and accountability in school board run for District B
item.description: Lydia Dominguez, candidate for the Clark County School Board in District B, joined the 8 News Now Daily Voting Guide to outline her vision for the district, focusing on financial accountability, student safety, and parental involvement.
item.dc:creator: Justin Walker, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 25 Pinellas County voting locations change after hurricanes
item.description: Twenty-five polling locations in Pinellas County have been changed due to hurricanes Helene and Milton, according to the county supervisor of elections.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: WATCH: Conversation with Kim Salas about the top issues in the Kern County Board of Supervisors District 5 race
item.description: Voters of the Kern County Board of Supervisors 5th District will have a chance tonight to get answers on topics that matter to them most.
item.dc:creator: Jose Franco, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump takes jabs at Harris during keynote at Al Smith dinner, vows to correct 'serious problems' in US
item.description: Former President Trump will headline the Al Smith Catholic charity dinner in New York on Thursday, after Vice President Kamala Harris snubbed the event and will send a video message instead.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Oklahoma politicians debate ethics of 'paddling' students
item.description: There were 351 reported instances of Oklahoma students with disabilities who were stricken in schools during the 2020-21 school year.
item.dc:creator: Markie Martin, NewsNation

item.title: Supreme Court to allow Biden admin power plant climate standards to remain in place – for now
item.description: The Supreme Court declined an emergency request to block Biden administration climate standards on power plant emissions.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US imposes sanctions on Chinese companies accused of helping make Russian attack drones
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The U.S. Treasury Department on Thursday announced sanctions against two Chinese companies accused of directly helping Russia build long-range attack drones used in the war in Ukraine. It is the first time the U.S.
item.dc:creator: DIDI TANG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Who was Yahya Sinwar? The Israeli prisoner turned terrorist Hamas leader killed by IDF troops
item.description: Sinwar rose to the top position after the killing of previous leader Ismail Haniyeh in the explosion of a guesthouse in Tehran on July 30.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: 5 takeaways from the last full fundraising quarter before Election Day
item.description: Federal candidates have submitted their reports for what was the final full fundraising quarter before Election Day, offering a snapshot of their financial status with just three weeks to go.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: The Latest: Harris keeps a focus on 'blue wall' states in the campaign's final weeks
item.description: With three weeks left in the presidential campaign, Democrat Kamala Harris is spending most of her days trying to shore up support in the “blue wall” states of Pennsylvania, Michigan and Wisconsin as she tries to avoid a repeat of Hillary Clinton’s collapse there eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance says 'no' when asked if Trump lost in 2020
item.description: The answer was the most specific Vance has been on the subject, and a departure from the series of evasions he has offered about the election Trump continues to say without evidence was decided fraudulently.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden administration forgives student loans for 60,000 public service workers
item.description: The Biden administration announced Thursday that it is forgiving the student loans for more than 60,000 public service workers. The move gives $4.5 billion in debt relief to teachers, firefighters and other eligible workers who have been paying on their loans for more than 10 years.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Trump tried a friendlier pitch with Latino voters at a Univision town hall
item.description: During a town hall outside Miami, Trump touted his record on the economy, yet called Jan. 6 a "day of love" and would not back off false claims about Haitian migrants eating pets in Springfield, Ohio.
item.dc:creator: Franco Ordoñez, MPR News

item.title: Harris Fox News interview draws sharp reactions on both sides
item.description: “If she wins, it will be because of that answer … which is ‘I’m not Joe Biden,’” columnist Chris Cillizza noted.
item.dc:creator: Rich Johnson, NewsNation

item.title: Florida elections officials seek flexibility on voting rules after hurricanes
item.description: Florida's recent hurricanes could make it difficult for voters in affected areas to cast their ballots in the upcoming election unless Gov. Ron DeSantis waives and modifies some rules, county election supervisors said in a letter to state officials.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Liz Cheney jumps into House races — for Democrats
item.description: Former Rep. Liz Cheney (R-Wyo.) is taking her anti-Trump crusade to the congressional battlefield, endorsing Democrats in a pair of contested House races in a sign that she sees the lower chamber as a potential check on former President Trump if he wins a second term in the White House.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Republican Party sues Hennepin County over election judges list
item.description: A state law requires each county in Minnesota select election judges from lists provided by the Republican and Democratic parties. A lawsuit claims Hennepin County violated state statute by not selecting judges from a GOP list.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Nebraska court allows voter registrations from people with felony convictions
item.description: Nebraskans with felony convictions may register to vote after the state's highest court ruled Wednesday that a top election official had no authority to deem a law restoring those rights unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: Republicans grapple with storm of misinformation from their own party
item.description: Republicans representing states hit by hurricanes Helene and Milton are grappling with the spread of storm misinformation, much of which has stemmed from prominent figures in their own party — including sitting lawmakers and former President Trump.
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Harris peppered with questions by Charlamagne tha God’s audience
item.description: Vice President Harris was peppered with questions Tuesday about her plan for Black Americans from radio host Charlamagne tha God and his listeners in an interview that’s part of a late-campaign media blitz before Election Day.
item.dc:creator: Cheyanne M. Daniels, NewsNation

item.title: Nevada DMV fast-tracks credentials for potential voters
item.description: The Nevada Department of Motor Vehicles is seeking to move potential voters to the front of the line starting next week and is reminding new residents they can surrender their out-of-state ID.
item.dc:creator: James Schaeffer, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Migrant deaths in New Mexico have increased tenfold
item.description: Experts say smugglers are treating migrants more harshly and bringing them on paths that could be more dangerous in extreme summer temperatures.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Giant pandas will arrive at D.C.'s National Zoo this week
item.description: After an 11-month wait, giant pandas will populate the zoo once again. A pair of pandas, which left China on FedEx's "Panda Express" cargo jet, are expected to arrive in the D.C. area on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Willem Marx, MPR News

item.title: NJ lawmakers' fight against teen vaping continues with new bill
item.description: TRENTON, N.J. (PIX11) -- The fight against youth vaping continues in the Garden State as some Trenton lawmakers are trying to pass a law prohibiting the delivery of electronic smoking devices to people under 21, requiring someone over 21 to sign off on the delivery.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Election 2024: Races to watch in New Jersey
item.description: NEW JERSEY (PX11) -- It's been one of the most notable years for New Jersey politics in recent memory — indictments, upsets, and loss are just some themes of what has been a year that could shape the Garden State's political future for years to come.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: Senate rankings: 5 seats most likely to flip
item.description: The battle for the Senate majority has hit its final month as Republicans race to end their four-year stint in the minority. The GOP needs to win only one of two competitive races in red states — Montana and Ohio — to do just that, barring a surprise loss elsewhere.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, Fox 5

item.title: Johnson: Additional hurricane aid 'can wait' until Congress is back in session
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson (R-La.) on Sunday said passing additional hurricane aid for states impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton “can wait” until Congress is back in session after the election.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Maria Torres-Springer discusses new role as NYC’s first deputy mayor
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday appointed Maria Torres-Springer to be New York City’s new first deputy mayor after Sheena Wright resigned from her position.
item.dc:creator: Henry Rosoff, PIX 11
